pages.raw_text:
That the present high price of
wheat is not a natural condition
and likely to result in ultimate in
jury to the growers is the opinion
of Charles S. Barrett, president of
the Farmers union, who has issued
a call to the members of the union
to meet at Springfield, Mo-. on May
IL to take such action as will pre-
vent similar conditions in the flu
ture.


The whole power of the union
will be thrown back of the move.
ment to place the price of wheat
on an enduring high basis. All
cattle raisers are included in the
call and an attempt will be made
to organize the cattle and wheat
industry into the same cohesion
and power that has been success
fully brought about by the union
in the case of the cotton growers.


The call includes members of the
union and cattle growers in the
state of Tennessee, Kentucky, In
diana, Missouri, Oklahoma, Kansas,
Washington, Oregon, California
Texas and Idaho.
